title: FDA Approves ELEVIDYS - First Gene Therapy to Treat Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy
text:
Sarepta Therapeutics announced FDA approval of ELEVIDYS (delandistrogene moxeparvovec-rokl), marking the first gene therapy approved to treat Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD). The FDA granted accelerated approval of the therapy for the treatment of ambulatory pediatric patients aged 4 through 5 years with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) with a confirmed mutation in the DMD gene.

Indicator and Market Impact: This approval represents a transformative treatment option for eligible patients with DMD. The gene therapy is now indicated for ambulatory patients aged 4 years and older following subsequent label expansions. Ongoing approval for patients who are nonambulatory may depend on the results of ENVISION (Study SRP-9001-303), a phase 3 confirmatory clinical trial.

Label Expansion: The June 2024 FDA approval expanded ELEVIDYS eligibility to over 80% of Duchenne patients in the U.S., significantly expanding the addressable patient population. This expansion made ELEVIDYS available to both ambulatory and, in certain circumstances, nonambulatory patients.

Safety Considerations: The ELEVIDYS label includes important safety information. An updated label approved in November 2025 includes a boxed warning for the risk of acute serious liver injury (ALI) and acute liver failure (ALF). The FDA has approved dosing in a study cohort to evaluate the use of immunosuppressive regimen (Sirolimus) as part of treatment with ELEVIDYS for patients who cannot walk.

Commercial Significance: Peak sales estimates for ELEVIDYS in the U.S. market range from $2.7 billion to over $5 billion annually, positioning it as a potentially transformative product for Sarepta.
